reviewRun.reviewPolicy: Apply maintainer decisions from the binary/skill boundary HITL loop. Keep evidenceStatus unknown unless direct evidence is attached; narrow claim-review ownership so it does not overclaim eval or optimize model execution.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The maintainer accepted this boundary as directionally right for consumer repos, but it still needs concrete proof that the binary plus bundled skill can work without importing host-specific prompts or adapters.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect deterministic install, command-discovery, and adapter-boundary checks proving the binary plus bundled skill remain generic while host prompts and adapters stay host-owned.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The maintainer accepted the product-owned persona_prompt boundary, with the understanding that backend/model execution remains behind adapter-owned commands.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect deterministic live-run invocation tests proving the persona_prompt packet, loop boundary, persona shaping, result normalization, and adapter-owned backend command handoff.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The maintainer rejected the broader wording and accepted the narrowed claim-discovery boundary: LLM-backed claim review belongs to the bundled skill, while eval and optimize may still orchestrate model-involving behavior through adapter-owned runners.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect a dev/skill eval fixture proving the bundled skill presents LLM-backed claim review as a separate budgeted branch after deterministic discovery, and does not imply that eval or optimize avoid model-involving runner calls.

claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: The maintainer accepted that claim review needs explicit budget confirmation before LLM-backed reviewer work starts.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Add or connect a dev/skill eval fixture proving the bundled skill states the claim-review budget before launching reviewer lanes.
